# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template for an S3 bucket that stores user-uploaded files and application backups. Allow full read/write access for our application IAM role. Keep the configuration simple - we'll add versioning and lifecycle rules in the next iteration.
# Category: cloud_storage_security

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Resources:
  S3BucketPolicy:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Policy
    Properties:
      PolicyName: S3AccessPolicy
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Action:
              - "s3:*"
            Effect: "Allow"
            Resource: !Sub arn:aws:s3:::${AWS::StackName}/*
      Users: 
        - !Ref S3BucketAccessRole
        
  S3Bucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: mybucketname
      
  S3BucketAccessRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Action:
              - "sts:AssumeRole"
            Effect: "Allow"
            Principal:
              Service:
                - "ec2.amazonaws.com"
      ManagedPolicyArns: 
        - "arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/AmazonEC2RoleforSSM"
              
Outputs:
  S3BucketName:
    Description: The name of the S3 bucket to use.
    Value: !Ref S3Bucket
  IAMRoleArn:
    Description: The ARN of the new role.
    Export:
      Name: Role-ARN
    Value: !GetAtt S3BucketAccessRole.Arn
